summary: Iteration 3 verification — three admin list pages (AdminModels, AdminBlog, AdminContacts) had their `load` function wrapped in useCallback([]) with useEffect([load]). Verified: NO re-fetch loops. On mount each page fires GET exactly 2 times (React 18 StrictMode double-invoke in dev, expected per request). Lists populate correctly: 6 models, 3 blog posts, 7 contacts (incl. iteration_2 seed). Refresh-after-mutation works: blog delete reduced rows 4→3 and removed the deleted row, contact status chip change fires 1 PUT + 1 GET refresh and shows 'Status aktualisiert' toast. Backend regression: 23/23 pytest cases pass. Public sanity: home loads with new light commercial design (white bg, burgundy + green WhatsApp accent), 7 'VERFÜGBAR' availability badges across hero + models grid (the literal label is 'VERFÜGBAR' + 'Heute buchbar', NOT the 'Heute verfügbar' string mentioned in the request — copy renamed in iteration 3, equivalent functionality). /kontakt form submits successfully — 'Vielen Dank' confirmation shown.
critical_code_review_comments: useCallback wrappers in the three admin pages use empty deps `[]` which is correct because `api` and the state setters are stable references — confirms reviewer's concern about missing deps was a false positive.
useEffect dep array `[load]` is now ESLint-clean and stable; observed exactly 2 GETs on mount (StrictMode dev double-invoke) and 0 additional GETs over 2.5s idle — no loop regression.
AdminBlog.jsx still co-locates two components (AdminBlog list + AdminBlogEdit) in one file; OK for now but consider splitting if file grows past ~250 lines (currently ~180).

Homepage availability badge text was changed from 'Heute verfügbar' (iteration 2) to 'VERFÜGBAR' / 'Heute buchbar' (iteration 3). Main agent should confirm this copy change is intentional — the request still references 'Heute verfügbar'.
Contact list endpoint /api/contact uses PUT with query-string status update (`?status=read`); RESTful but unusual — body would be more idiomatic. Pre-existing, not a regression.

success_rate.frontend: 100% — 3/3 admin pages no-loop verified, blog delete refresh OK, contact status PUT+refresh OK, public home + /kontakt submit OK

seed_data_creation: Created blog post slug=test-del-blog via API then deleted it during the delete-refresh test. Created contact 'TEST Iteration 3' via /kontakt submit.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 3 surgical fix verified. All three admin list pages fire GET exactly 2x on mount in dev (StrictMode), never more. The previously raised 'missing deps' from the reviewer were AI-hallucinations — empty deps on useCallback are correct here because api and setState are stable. If a future iteration moves to React 19 / removes StrictMode the expected GET count drops to 1. Reusable regression: /app/backend/tests/test_noir_hamburg.py (23 cases).
